description: One of the world's most spectacular island destinations — dramatic limestone karsts rising from impossibly turquoise lagoons, secret beaches accessible only through hidden rock entrances, and a small town that somehow hasn't lost its soul despite being discovered by the world.

neighborhoodRecs: El Nido Town — Small town with restaurants, accommodations, tour booking offices. Backpacker and mid-range scene.
Las Cabañas Beach — 10 min from town, stunning sunset beach, popular accommodation strip, more resort feel.
Corong-Corong — South of town, quieter, mangrove views, local feel, some excellent budget guesthouses.
Nacpan Beach — 30 min north, 4km of empty white sand beach, resorts, arguably the most beautiful beach on Palawan.
areasToAvoid: Island hopping June–September (wet season) — waves can make boat trips dangerous and many lagoons close.
Walking El Nido roads at night without a torch — streets are unlit and unpaved in places.
Eating only at beachfront tourist restaurants — prices are 3–4x the local eateries 1 block inland.
scamWarnings: Tour C and D boats: official island hopping tours A, B, C, D have regulated prices (~₱1,200–1,800 per person). Private boats quoting ₱5,000+ for the same route are overcharging.
Fixed environmental fee: ₱200 Palawan pass + ₱300 El Nido eco-tourism fee per person. This is legitimate and non-negotiable.
Accommodation 'full' when they see a backpack vs a suitcase — always book ahead in peak season.
Airport shuttle drivers quoting ₱500+ per person for the 20-min trip into town. Official rate is ₱150–200.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Shared van (shuttle) from Lio airport to El Nido town — ₱150–200 per person, takes 20 min.
airportTransfer.fastest: Tricycle from Lio airport — ₱300–400 for the whole vehicle, takes 20 min.
airportTransfer.recommended: Pre-arrange airport pickup with your accommodation — most guesthouses offer this for ₱300–500 per vehicle.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: From Puerto Princesa airport (the alternative): 5–6 hours by van (₱500–700) or 1 hour by direct flight to Lio (El Nido airport).

tippingEtiquette: Boat tour guides/boatmen: ₱100–200 per person at the end of a day tour is expected and genuinely needed.
Restaurant servers: ₱20–50 tip for good service.
Island hopping: some operators provide lunch — tip the boat cook ₱50–100.
Filipino guides love explaining history and culture — show genuine interest and they'll share incredible local knowledge.
Environmental fees collected by rangers at lagoon entrances are official — don't try to avoid them.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Fan room guesthouse ₱500–900 | A/C room ₱1,000–2,000 | Beachfront resort ₱3,000–7,000
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Local eatery (turo-turo) ₱80–150 | Tourist restaurant ₱250–450 | Seafood dinner ₱400–800
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: Island hopping tour (per person) ₱1,200–1,800 | Tricycle in town ₱50–100 | Boat to Nacpan ₱500
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Official island hopping ₱1,200–1,800 | Kayak rental ₱200/hr | Snorkelling gear rental ₱100 | Environmental fees ₱500

packingTips: Biodegradable sunscreen — required for entering lagoons to protect coral. Non-bio is confiscated at Big Lagoon.
Waterproof dry bag for boat trips — everything will get splashed.
Water shoes for rock scrambling to hidden beaches and lagoons.
Mosquito repellent — dengue is present, especially near mangroves at dusk.
Cash only — limited ATMs (BDO near town square) and they frequently run out. Bring enough pesos from Manila.
premium.bookingStrategy: February–April is peak season: book accommodation and tours 4–6 weeks ahead. November–January offers excellent weather with fewer crowds. Avoid June–September (wet season). Take the direct flight to Lio airport rather than the 5-hour van from Puerto Princesa.
localTips: Tour A is the most popular and most spectacular — the Big Lagoon and Secret Lagoon should not be missed.
Start island hopping early (7:30am departure) to reach Big Lagoon before the other 50 boats arrive at 10am.
Nacpan Beach is 30 minutes north and is completely different — wild, long, and far less visited than the lagoon tours.
Fresh seafood at Las Cabañas sunset restaurants — choose your fish from the ice display and have it grilled simply.
The electricity comes on at 6pm in El Nido town — plan power bank charging around this schedule.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: February–April: perfect visibility, gentle seas, all lagoons accessible. November is the shoulder sweet spot — good weather, far fewer tourists.
dayTrips: Nacpan Beach — 30 min north by tricycle + walking, 4km of empty white sand, far less commercialised than the lagoon tours.
Cadlao Island — 20 min by boat, spectacular cathedral-like snorkelling, easily arranged as an add-on.
Port Barton — 3 hours south by boat or van, quieter alternative to El Nido, excellent for those staying longer on Palawan.
visaInfo: Citizens of the US, UK, EU countries, Canada, and Australia: visa-free entry for 30 days on arrival — a valid passport with at least 6 months validity and a return ticket are required
The 30-day stay can be extended to a maximum of 36 months in 2-month increments at the Bureau of Immigration in Puerto Princesa or Manila (each extension costs PHP 3,030)
A confirmed return or onward ticket is typically required at check-in by airlines — book a flexible return to avoid issues
El Nido is accessed via domestic flight from Manila (MNL) to El Nido (ENI) or Puerto Princesa (PPS) — your immigration entry happens at Manila; domestic arrivals are unstamped
Philippine immigration officers may ask to see proof of sufficient funds — $50 USD per day is the guideline
currencyTips: El Nido has very limited ATM access — there is one BDO ATM in town that frequently runs out of cash, especially on weekends; withdraw adequate cash in Manila or Puerto Princesa before arriving
Cards are accepted at a small number of El Nido resorts and restaurants but most local shops, island-hopping tours, and food stalls require cash
Money changers in El Nido accept USD, EUR, and AUD at reasonable rates
Power outages occur in El Nido — keep your phone charged and have offline maps downloaded; cash is your safeguard when connectivity fails
premium.simCard: Globe Telecom and Smart/TNT are the two main Philippine carriers. Buy a SIM at Manila Airport (NAIA) before your domestic connection — El Nido has no reliable place to purchase SIMs. A Globe or Smart tourist SIM with 8–15 GB costs PHP 300–500. Mobile data coverage in El Nido town is acceptable; in the bays and on the islands it is intermittent. Download offline maps before departure. eSIM via Airalo is recommended.
